afjEngineEnabled = false must stop the engine from ever engaging, on any page

The real trigger for this feature: the engine was firing on the extension's own distribution site (a job-hunting SaaS's own /settings page) because that page happened to look job-application-shaped by the content gate's heuristics. A path-based fix closes that specific case, but the user also asked for a manual override — this verifies the popup's toggle actually gates the engine's entry point, not just a cosmetic setting.

running…
